title: Apex Dev Orchestrator Plan
description: Coordinator output for the dev-orchestrator skill. Orders Features into execution waves using feature-level edges only. Contains NO item-level (PBI/TBI) edges — item dependencies are intra-Feature by construction and irrelevant to Feature ordering.

slug.description: Kebab-slug matching the source backlog file.

sourceBacklog.description: Filename of the backlog JSON used as input (e.g. {slug}.backlog.json).

sourceTestCases.description: Filename of the optional test-cases JSON used to catalog deferred e2e. null when absent.

features.description: Feature nodes in the dependency graph.

waves.description: Ordered execution waves. Wave 1 executes first; a wave may contain multiple Features that are safe to build in parallel.

syncPoints.description: Wave-boundary sync points. Downstream Feature branches are cut only after all upstream Features have merged.

conflicts.description: Phase/DAG conflicts flagged by the coordinator: a feature.dependsOn edge whose ordering disagrees with implementationPhases. Empty array when none.

deferredE2E.description: Test cases with automation.recommendedTier === 'e2e-playwright' — cataloged and skipped by the Feature Executor. Empty array when none.

epic.description: Title of the parent epic this Feature belongs to.

phase.description: implementationPhases phase number assigned to this Feature's parent epic.

dependsOn.description: FEATURE-LEVEL edges only. Feature IDs this Feature depends on. Empty array if none. No item-level (PBI/TBI) edges are ever recorded here.

wave.description: 1-based wave number. Lower numbers execute first.

featureIds.description: Features in this wave — safe to build in parallel.

rationale.description: Why these Features share a wave, or why the wave must follow the previous one.

afterWave.description: The wave whose Features must all merge before the next wave's branches are cut.

mustMergeFeatureIds.description: Features that must be merged at this sync point.

detail.description: Explanation, e.g. 'FEAT-002 dependsOn FEAT-003 but is in an earlier or equal phase — reconcile in /to-prd.'
